title: malus × purpurea var. aldenhamensis
intro: Malus × purpurea var. aldenhamensis, also known as the Aldenham Crab Apple, is a rare and unusual variety of crab apple tree. This deciduous tree has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gastrointestinal issues. The bark, leaves, and fruit of the Aldenham Crab Apple tree are used to prepare herbal remedies, which are said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the Aldenham Crab Apple can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
